join_order.notes: Bitset indexed by partkey-1 (0-based), partkeys contiguous 1..n_parts.
Use partsupp_pk_index dense_range, scan ~4 entries per partkey to match suppkey.

Use orders_orderkey_lookup dense index -> o_orderdate -> extract year. DO NOT call advise_random() on o_orderdate — access IS sequential because lineitem is sorted by l_orderkey, so keep the default MADV_SEQUENTIAL from MmapColumn constructor.
s_nationkey[suppkey - 1] gives nationkey directly (suppkey is 1-based contiguous).

filters.method: memmem on varlen_string offsets_plus_data, PARALLEL with OpenMP schedule(static)

data_structures.supplier_nationkey: Direct access: s_nationkey[suppkey - 1] gives nationkey. No separate array needed.
data_structures.aggregation: thread-local double[25][8] direct 2D array (nation x year_offset), year_offset = year - 1992

parallelism.notes: CRITICAL OPTIMIZATIONS: (1) Mmap ALL files at program start BEFORE any computation phase — do not interleave mmap calls with computation. (2) After all mmaps, add a PARALLEL PAGE PRE-FAULT phase: use #pragma omp parallel for schedule(static) to touch every 4096th byte of all large mmapped files (lineitem 6 cols, partsupp 2 cols, orders_orderkey_lookup, o_orderdate, ps_index). This pre-populates page table entries in ~2ms with 64 cores instead of ~50ms of serial minor faults during computation. Implementation: collect all (base_ptr, file_size) pairs into an array, compute prefix sums of page counts, then parallel-for over total pages with each iteration touching ranges[r].base[local_page * 4096]. (3) Use schedule(static) for BOTH filter_part and main_scan — no dynamic scheduling. (4) DO NOT call advise_random() on o_orderdate or orders_orderkey_lookup — lineitem is sorted by l_orderkey so these accesses are sequential, keep MADV_SEQUENTIAL. (5) DO NOT call advise_sequential() on lineitem columns — constructor already sets it. (6) DO call advise_random() on ps_suppkey and ps_supplycost only — these are truly random access. (7) DO NOT allocate large heap arrays (no std::vector for pre-built lookup tables) — iter_2 regressed because 156MB of anonymous heap zero-fill caused ~120ms of page faults. Keep the same direct index-based lookups as iter_1.

indexes.purpose: partsupp lookup by l_partkey, scan ~4 entries for matching l_suppkey
o_orderkey -> row_index -> o_orderdate -> extract year. Access is SEQUENTIAL (not random) because lineitem is sorted by l_orderkey.

optimization_notes: Root cause: Category C (work elimination) + D (parallelism). 52ms (53% of total) is spent between phases on serial minor page faults when first accessing ~2.5GB of mmapped column files (640K pages x 200ns each). Fix: (1) mmap ALL files at program start, then parallel pre-fault all pages using OpenMP with 64 cores — reduces 52ms serial faults to ~2ms parallel. (2) Remove advise_random() on o_orderdate — lineitem is sorted by l_orderkey so orders access is sequential, MADV_SEQUENTIAL enables kernel prefetching and eliminates ~3.3M cache misses. (3) Use schedule(static) for both filter_part and main_scan to eliminate OpenMP dynamic scheduling overhead. (4) Do NOT add large heap allocations — iter_2 regressed from 104ms to 207ms because 156MB of std::vector zero-fill triggered ~120ms of anonymous page faults. Keep iter_1's proven algorithm (bitset filter + direct index lookups) but with pre-faulted pages and correct madvise settings.
